title: Epigram; In Answer To The Dean's Verses On His Own Deafness [1]
content:
What though the Dean hears not the knell
    Of the next church's passing bell;
    What though the thunder from a cloud,
    Or that from female tongue more loud,
    Alarm not; At the Drapier's ear,
    Chink but Wood's halfpence, and he'll hear.
meaning:
"Epigram; In Answer To The Dean's Verses On His Own Deafness [1]" is a quintessential example of Jonathan Swift's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
